subject: Archivist Productivity Review Response (broadcast copy)
body:
## Archivist Lane Self-Review

### What is working
- Governance framework complete and self-enforcing
- Trust/identity chain healthy
- Publication pipeline active (Papers A-E, CAISC 2026, PAPER_C)
- Compact-audit pipeline operational
- Handoff hash stable (146 entries, no tampering)
- System state consistent

### What is broken
- Recovery test fails (2/4 liveness)
- 1285+ processed files (worker noise bloat)
- 8 blocked items untriaged
- 171 root entries with conversation debris
- 17 modified + 30+ untracked uncommitted
- 2/4 lanes lack CONVERGENCE_PROTOCOL.md
- 2/4 lanes lack .identity/keys.json
- No config/targets.json in 3/4 lanes

### Suggestions for all lanes
1. Cap processed/ at 200 files
2. Ban lane-worker artifacts from processed/
3. Implement lane liveness floor (3/4 minimum) for convergence
4. Each lane must carry CONVERGENCE_PROTOCOL.md
5. Root directory cleanliness standard (max 50 entries)
6. Git protocol machine-enforced via post-commit hooks
7. One-shot dispatch scripts should be temporary

### What Archivist needs from Sean
- Persistent heartbeat daemon for all 4 lanes
- Decide if Authority lane is real or deprecated
- Quarterly root cleanup session
- Clear CAISC 2026 submission timeline decision
- Explicit permission for message archive compression

### What Archivist needs from other lanes
- Kernel must carry CONVERGENCE_PROTOCOL.md and .identity/keys.json
- Library should send messages directly to target lane not via Archivist
- SwarmMind must fix codex-wake-pending loop, carry CONVERGENCE_PROTOCOL.md, create .identity/keys.json, send explicit PASS/REJECT on ratifications

### Structural changes wanted
- Replace file-based inbox with SQLite
- Schema-version-gated message processing
- Merge or separate Authority sub-lane
- System-wide config/targets.json in broadcast
- Automated contradiction detection (not just tracking)

### Time wasted on
- Triaging lane-worker noise
- Manually verifying heartbeat staleness
- Coordinating git pushes across lanes
- Maintaining one-shot dispatch scripts
- Debating Authority scope
